mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC between monthly PAYEMS change and 20-day SPX forward returns is negative or not significantly different from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy begins to curb credit growth and economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% during any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an aggressive central bank response to inflation that outpaces nominal earnings growth, compressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS does not significantly predict negative 20-day SPX returns (t-stat < 1.5) in a 5-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) generates positive short-term equity returns specifically during periods of strong labor markets, as wage-backed consumption becomes the primary driver of earnings surprises.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 5-day SPX returns when UNRATE < 5.0% over a 3-year rolling window (hit rate < 55%).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below zero predicts negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter as earnings recession risk rises.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth coincides with positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unexpected declines in housing starts (HOUST) signal a contraction in residential investment that reduces demand for construction, durable goods, and mortgage credit, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative HOUST surprises coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) predicts negative S&P 500 returns as lower household income growth triggers downward earnings revisions.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases > 0.2pp do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals durable consumer demand that supports corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to produce positive Rank IC with 60-day SPX forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.
